sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) [const. דְּבַר; suff. דְּבָרִי; pl. דְּבָרִים, const. דִּבְרֵי], word, λόγος, Gen 44:18, etc. Often in pl. Gen 29:13, כָּל־הַדְּבָרִים הָאֵלֶּה “all those words;” Gen 34:18 Exo 4:28 18:19 19:7 , Exo 19:8 20:1 24:3 , etc.; בַּעַל דְּבָרִים, אִישׁ an eloquent man, Exo 4:10 24:14 . Often collect. words, speech, discourse, Job 15:3 דְּבַר שְׂפָתַיִם word of lips, i.e. futile, vain speech, Isa 36:5 נְבֹון דָּבָר skilled in speech, 1Sa 16:18. Specially it is-(a) a promise, something promised, 1Ki 2:4 8:20 12:16 Psa 33:4 56:5 (compare Greek τελεῖν ἔπος, Germ. fein Wort halten). (b) a precept, an edict (compare דִּבֶּר No. 1, b); דְּבַר־מַלְכוּת a royal mandate, Est 1:19 Jos 1:13 1Sa 17:29, הֲלֹא דָּבָר הוּא “was there not a commandment?” Isa 8:10 Exo 34:28, עֲשֶׂרֶת הַדְּבָרִים the ten commandments, the decalogue, 1Ch 26:32 2Ch 29:15.-(c) a saying, a sentence, as of a wise man; pl. Ecc 1:1, דִּבְרֵי קֹהֶלֶת Pro 4:4, 20 30:1 31:1 especially the word of the Lord, an oracle, Num 23:5, 16 Num 23:16 (compare ἔπος, λόγος); וַיְהִי דְבַר יְהֹוָה אֶל the word of Jehovah came to any one, Jer 1:4, 11 2:1 13:8 Eze 3:16 6:1 7:1 11:14 followed by עַל 1Ch 22:8 Job 4:12, אֵלַי דָּבָר יְנֻנַּב “a (divine) oracle was secretly brought to me.” Very often collect. oracles, Hos 1:1 Mic 1:1 Joe 1:1.-(d) a counsel, proposed plan, 2Sa 17:6.-(e) rumour, report, 1Ki 10:6 followed by a genit. words to be spoken concerning anything, what is to be said about it; Job 41:4, “I will be silent … דְּבַר גְּבוּרֹות as to what is to be said about his strength;” 1Ki 9:15 Deu 15:2 19:4 . It may also be rendered, what is the measure of his strength (compare דִּבְרָה No. 1).

(2) thing, thing done, affair, business, prop. that which is spoken of (compare λόγος in Passow, A. No. 11, ἔπος, ῥῆμα from ῤέω, Germ. Sache from fagen, Ding, which originally signified a discourse; see Adelung, h. v. The same power of word and thing is conjoined in the Aram. מִלָּה, ܡܶܠܬܐܳ, פִּתְנָּם, Arabic أَمْرُ and خَطْبُ). דִּבְרֵי שְׁלמֹה the actions of Solomon, the notable deeds, 1Ki 11:41 דִּבְרֵי הַיָמִים commentaries of actions performed, journals, 1Ch 27:24 Est 6:1 הַדָּבָר הַוֶּה this thing, this, Gen 20:10 21:11 , 26 Gen 21:26כָּל־הַדְּבָרִים הָאֵלֶּה all these things, Gen 20:8 כַּדָּבָר הַזֶּח Gen 18:25 32:20 44:7 and הָאֵלֶּה כַּדְּבָרִים Gen 24:28 39:17 , 19 Gen 39:19in this manner, thus, אַהַר הַדְּבָרִים הָאֵלֶּה (LXX. μετὰ τὰ ῥήματα ταῦτα), after these things, when they were accomplished, Gen 15:1 22:1 39:7 דְּבַר יֹום daily matter; hence דְּבַר יֹום בְּיֹומֹו a daily matter in its day, i.e. daily, day by day, Exo 5:13, 19 16:4 Lev 23:37 1Ki 8:59 also בִּרְבַר יֹום בְּיֹום 2Ch 8:13 and לִרְבַר יֹום בְּיֹומֹו verse 14 2Ch 8:14 31:16 . More often it stands pleon. like the Gr. χρήμα; 1Sa 10:2, נָטַשׁ אָבִיךָ אֶת־דִּבְרֵי הָאֲתֹנֹות “thy father has left the matters of the asses,” has ceased to care for them; prop. die Angelegenheit, die Gefchichte mit den Efelinnen; Psa 65:4, דִּבְרֵי עֲֹונֹות גָּֽבְרוּ מֶגִּי “iniquities prevail against me;” Psa 105:27 145:5 . Hence-

(3) anything, something, Gen 18:14 לֹא דָבָר אֵין דָּבָר nothing; 1Sa 20:21, אֵין דָּבָר “there is nothing,” sc. to fear; Jud. 18:7 , 28 Jdg 18:28, וְרָבָר אֵין־לָהֶם עִם־אָדָם “and they had no concern (or business) with (other) men;” כָּל־דָּבָר everything, anything, Num 31:23 Deu 17:1 דָּבָר טָמֵא anything unclean, Lev 5:2 עֶרְוַת דָּבָר anything filthy, Deu 23:15 24:1 compare 2Ki 4:41 1Sa 20:2. Also pl. דְּבָרִים טֹובִים 2Ch 12:12.

(4) a cause, reason, Jos 5:4. Hence עַל דְּבַר on account of, Gen 12:17 20:11 43:18 עַל דִּבְרֵי id., Deu 4:21 Jer 7:22 14:1 עַל דְּבַר אֲשֶׁר followed by a verb, because that, Deu 22:24 23:5 2Sa 13:22 (compare דִּבְרָה No. 2).

(5) cause, in a forensic sense; Exo 18:16, לָהֶם דָּבָר כִּי־יִהְיֶה “if they have a cause,” verse 22 Exo 18:22 22:8 , עַל־כָּל־דָּבַר־פֶּשַׁע “in every cause (suit) of trespass;” Exo 24:14, בַּעַל דְּבָרִים “one who has causes, suits.”

[דְּבָר see pr.n. לֹא דְּבָר .]
